text: People from very different walks of life learn C nowadays, so courses are hard to balance, especially when C is just means to an end. One audience member may be setting and clearing bits for the first time in their life while another wants to know how to store the address of the current instruction and jump to it later, like they do in assembler all the time. Sometimes it's possible to make stricter entrance tests, other times we have to limit the group size to ensure that the instructor/student ratio is high enough. Then we can help the people who fall behind while keeping others entertained with advanced assignments (e.g. on encodings and why Cyrillic characters have trouble fitting into one `char`).
